planDocSection: §8 Process Safeguards S1–S6 + §13.2 reorder approval (ships BEFORE M-C/M-D) + §14 Sprint Execution Framework
title: Phase 5.6 M-E T0 — Ship the S1–S6 process-safeguards suite (milestone DoD template + CI conformance check + pre-commit claim-evidence hook + Loki `verifiedBy` field + branch policy + quarterly re-audit cadence)
status: queued — head-of-queue for the Phase 5.6 sprint sequence
predecessor: Phase 5.6 M-B (Triage) SHIPPED 2026-04-17 as docs/audits/2026-04-17-conformance-audit.md amendment — atomic commit 6b5d481 + paired ledger. Dispositions: 27 restore / 14 deprecate / 0 replace across 41 distinct gap rows. Cluster A (multi-company M7) and Cluster B (org chart M9) both confirmed RESTORE with per-row justification + M-C/M-D owner assignment. §17 cross-check 10 / 10 rows confirmed zero diffs from M-A matrix. §20 rollup authoritative for M-C/M-D/M-E/M-F scoping.
goal: Land the six-point process-safeguards suite (S1 milestone DoD template, S2 CI conformance check, S3 pre-commit claim-evidence hook, S4 Loki `verifiedBy` field on ledger entries, S5 branch policy gate, S6 quarterly re-audit cadence) per plan §8. Safeguards ship BEFORE M-C/M-D backfill per approved §13.2 reorder — the restoration then runs under live CI gates + pre-commit enforcement + DoD template as the safeguards' first proof.

acceptance: S1 — NEW milestone DoD template (docs/templates/milestone-dod.md) enforcing claim-evidence-verification chain; usable as per-milestone exit checklist by M-C + M-D and every future phase
S2 — NEW CI conformance check (.github/workflows/conformance.yml or similar) that parses CLAUDE.md claims + greps the codebase for evidence + fails PR if a claim lacks on-disk evidence
S3 — NEW pre-commit claim-evidence hook (.husky/pre-commit or equivalent) running S2's local fast-path on every commit touching CLAUDE.md
S4 — `verifiedBy` field added to .loki/queue schemas + enforced by the Loki harness at milestone-completion time; future ledger entries record which evidence artifact proves the milestone shipped
S5 — branch policy documented in CONTRIBUTING.md — stranded-branch preservation rules + cherry-pick contract + merge-before-delete workflow so future M7/M9-style drift cannot recur
S6 — quarterly re-audit cadence documented with calendar triggers (next audit = 2026-07-17) + responsible-owner + Phase 5.6-style remediation playbook so drift is caught at ≤3-month lag

Each safeguard has a positive-control test (S1 template applied to a dry-run milestone; S2 CI run on a seeded-drift commit; S3 pre-commit run on a seeded-drift local change; S4 Loki harness dry-run; S5 branch-policy doc reviewed; S6 re-audit calendar entry created)

commitTemplate: feat(phase-5.6-m-e): Phase 5.6 M-E — process safeguards S1–S6 shipped

verification.unitTests: ≥1192/1192 pass (1187 baseline + ≥5 safeguard positive-control tests; ABI rebuild required)
verification.safeguards: every S1–S6 deliverable committed + positive-control test passing + documented in CONTRIBUTING.md
verification.ciGate: S2 conformance check green on the M-E atomic commit itself (the safeguard MUST pass on the commit that lands it)
verification.abi: Node + Electron ABI rebuild recipe from CLAUDE.md Troubleshooting executed before verification gate

notes: M-E is the PROCESS milestone — not feature work. Its deliverables make Phase 5.6 M-C + M-D + future phases impossible to drift under, by catching claim-vs-evidence divergence automatically in CI + pre-commit + Loki ledger + quarterly cadence.
Time-box is 2–3 working days + 1 day buffer per plan §4. M-E is the gate before M-C/M-D start any code-restoration work — safeguards ship FIRST so the backfill is their first proof.

S2 CI conformance check design note: parse CLAUDE.md IPC Channels table + bus-events table + status-block feature claims; grep for matching evidence (handler registrations for IPC; EventType union members for bus events; file / symbol / class / migration presence for features). Fail PR if any claim lacks on-disk evidence. Output: structured JSON log listing claim / expected-location / evidence / status — human-readable on GitHub Actions summary.
S3 pre-commit hook is a fast-path of S2 scoped to only the claims that changed in the current commit's CLAUDE.md diff — keeps local commits snappy.

S4 verifiedBy field: the Loki harness already records commits + test counts. Adding verifiedBy forces the LLM-executor to name the concrete evidence artifact (test file path, spec path, migration file path, CI run URL) that proves the milestone shipped — reducing ambiguity in future audits.

S5 branch policy: the root-cause of Cluster A + B drift was a stranded `worktree-phase-2-the-org` branch that was assumed merged but wasn't. Policy locks in: (a) no branch deletion without merge-verify; (b) milestone-completion ledger entry names the merge commit; (c) quarterly stranded-branch sweep.

S6 re-audit cadence: Phase 5.6 was a 6-8 week remediation because drift accumulated unchecked for 10+ milestones. Quarterly re-audit (Team-X's first cycle lands 2026-07-17 = 3 months after this M-E ship) catches drift at ≤3-month lag so future remediations are days, not weeks.
Do NOT start M-C (backend backfill) before M-E ships. The §13.2 reorder approval is explicit: safeguards ship BEFORE backfill, so the backfill is their first proof.

If S2 CI check fails on the M-E atomic commit itself, M-E is NOT shippable until the CI run is green — the safeguard must pass on the commit that lands it. This is the positive-control discipline: the safeguard's own introduction is its first gate.

Tests delta estimate is ≥5 (one positive-control test per safeguard minimum). Actual could be higher if the claim-evidence parser + grep engine needs unit coverage (recommended 10–15 tests to pin parser behaviour for IPC / bus-event / status-block claim shapes).

After M-E lands: head-of-queue advances to M-C (Backend Backfill) per plan §8 sequence. M-C tackles the 15 P0 RESTORE rows (Cluster A 7 + Cluster B 8) + selected P1/P2 RESTORE rows per audit §20.2 / §20.3.
